report_name: Everest Ransomware Claims Attacks on Citizens and Frost Banks

threat_actors.name: Everest
threat_actors.description: A ransomware gang that utilizes double-extortion tactics. The group claimed responsibility for cyberattacks against Citizens Financial Group and Frost Bank, threatening to leak stolen data on its dark web site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victims through data encryption and the threat of public data leaks.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Used to gain initial access to Citizens Bank by compromising one of its third-party vendors.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Cited as a typical technique used by ransomware groups like Everest to exfiltrate valuable data before deploying the encryption payload.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a likely technique for exfiltrating stolen data to cloud storage or other web services.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The final stage of the ransomware operation, where the Everest payload encrypts victim data to disrupt operations and force payment.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Used to prevent easy recovery from the attack by deleting volume shadow copies using tools like vssadmin.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Used to disable endpoint security tools such as antivirus or EDR solutions to allow the ransomware encryptor to run unimpeded.

malware.name: Everest Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Everest group to encrypt victim files and exfiltrate data as part of a double-extortion scheme.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by attackers to delete volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery.

tools.name: wbadmin
tools.description: Used by attackers to delete the backup catalog to prevent system recovery.

tools.name: net
tools.description: Used by attackers to stop services, particularly security tools like antivirus and EDR.

identities.name: Citizens Financial Group
identities.description: A major U.S. bank listed on the Everest ransomware gang's leak site. The bank confirmed a data breach involving a third-party vendor.

identities.name: Frost Bank
identities.description: A major U.S. bank listed on the Everest ransomware gang's leak site. The group claimed to have stolen data for 250,000 of the bank's clients, although this remains unconfirmed.

infrastructure.name: Everest Extortion Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web leak site used by the Everest ransomware gang to list victims and threaten the public release of stolen data to pressure them into paying a ransom.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8b3235-01e4-4309-8324-42b39e31f0e6
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/two-major-us-banks-targeted-citizens-bank-confirms-breach-frost-bank-allegedly-hit/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-22
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare for deleting all shadows.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP mentioned as a hunting hint in the article.

indicators.name: Wbadmin Backup Catalog Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Wbadmin Backup Catalog Deletion
id: 9c8d7e6f-5a4b-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wbadmin.exe to delete the backup catalog, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/two-major-us-banks-targeted-citizens-bank-confirms-breach-frost-bank-allegedly-hit/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-22
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wbadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'catalog'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity for decommissioning a backup.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of wbadmin.exe to delete the backup catalog, a common ransomware TTP mentioned as a hunting hint in the article.

indicators.name: Everest Ransom Note Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Everest Ransom Note Creation
id: 1e2f3a4b-5c6d-7e8f-9a0b-1c2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'RANSOM_NOTE.txt', which is a common indicator for ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://cybernews.com/news/two-major-us-banks-targeted-citizens-bank-confirms-breach-frost-bank-allegedly-hit/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-22
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\RANSOM_NOTE.txt'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely.
level: critical
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the Everest ransom note file as identified in the hunting hints.
